summary: FASE Cost Observability tested end-to-end. Backend: 22/22 pytest passing across 19 endpoints (overview/chart/providers/entities top+by-type/logs +filters provider+status/latency/pricing GET+POST override/budgets GET+POST/alerts open+run-checks/balance/backfill/alert-config GET+POST/export CSV) + auth-required gate + resend in tools catalog. Frontend: /admin/seo/cost-observatory loads cleanly post-login with all 8 stat cards visible (Today $0.0840, Week $2.07, Month $2.07, Forecast $7.75, Calls today 5, Success 89.7%, Avg latency 2227ms, Top provider deepl). Provider table renders 5 rows (deepl, claude, gemini, perplexity, dataforseo) with cost/budget bar (4.2%/1.2%/1.1%/0.5%/0.1% green) + colored success rate (perplexity 69.6% RED with 7 fails — matches mock data). Header buttons (Run alert check / Check balance / Export CSV / Email-SMTP / Backfill 30g / Refresh) all present and functional via testids. Budget modal opens listing 5 configured budgets (claude=$50, perplexity=$30, deepl=$20, gemini=$40, dataforseo=$10) + 'Aggiungi/Modifica' form. Email/SMTP modal opens with SMTP fields + 5 alert-type checkboxes. Alert engine run-checks returned 200 with 0 alerts (mock data spread across 10 days; intermittent threshold 30% over 24h window not triggered — verify in main agent if alert dispatch on backfilled data is expected).

minor.issue: Returns 'forecast_month_usd' (frontend consumes correctly). Field name inconsistent with mention 'forecast' in problem statement — minor naming drift, not a bug.

On current mock data (1 RATE_LIMIT failure, ~69% success on perplexity in /providers view) the engine reports new_alerts_count=0. Likely cause: API_INTERMITTENT threshold uses 24h window but mock data spans 10d so <30% fails fall in 24h. Verify if alerting on aggregated month should be added or if mock data should concentrate in 24h.

ui_bugs.issue: Only 3 of 8 stat cards expose data-testid (stat-today, stat-month, stat-forecast). Missing on Stat 'Week', 'Calls today', 'Success rate', 'Avg latency', 'Top provider' — should be stat-week, stat-calls, stat-success, stat-latency, stat-top-provider. Cards render correctly visually with values; testids needed for automation per problem statement.

issues: Pre-existing React hydration warnings persist (<span> in <select>/<option>/<tr>/<tbody>) — already documented in iteration_12. Not specific to Cost Observatory but visible in console here too.

action_items: (LOW) Add missing data-testid attributes to 5 stat cards in CostObservatory.jsx: stat-week, stat-calls, stat-success, stat-latency, stat-top-provider (problem statement explicitly required all 8 cards testable).

(VERIFY) Alert engine returns 0 new alerts on current mock data despite perplexity showing 69.6% success rate / 7 fails in monthly view. Confirm intent: API_INTERMITTENT (30% fail rate, 24h window) is correct; if so, mock data should include >3 perplexity failures within last 24h to demonstrate the alert path. Otherwise widen window or also evaluate monthly aggregate.
(LOW) Field naming: backend returns 'forecast_month_usd', problem statement / docstring mention 'forecast'. Alias for clarity if external consumers expected 'forecast'.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/backend/routes/cost_observatory.py: alert-config GET correctly hides smtp_pass and exposes smtp_pass_set + resend_key_set flags — good security pattern. POST also avoids overwriting smtp_pass on empty input — good.
Pricing POST endpoint accepts arbitrary provider/op_type strings without validation against a known list. Consider a Literal/Enum to prevent typos seeding bad pricing rows.
Backfill endpoint capped at days<=90, good. But /backfill returns full result synchronously — if seo_jobs collection grows large this can timeout. Consider 202 + background job pattern.
Export CSV streams via io.StringIO buffered (not true streaming). For 30+ days x many providers this could consume RAM. Convert to async generator yielding rows for true stream.
CostObservatory.jsx Stat component pattern reused with optional testid prop; only first 3 cards passed it — easy fix, just propagate testid={'stat-...'} to remaining 5.

Budget bar color logic uses budget_status field from backend (exceeded/warning/ok). Confirm /providers endpoint always populates this even when monthly_limit_usd is null (currently appears to fall back to bg-emerald-500 implicit — verify on a provider with no budget configured).

success_rate.frontend: 95% (8/8 stat cards visible + all modals work; 5 missing testids only)

seed_data_creation: Set perplexity budget to $30/80% via POST /budgets/perplexity. Set claude pricing override (sub_type=test_sonnet) via POST /pricing — TEST_pricing_override note. Saved alert-config doc with example SMTP config (smtp.example.com).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Cost Observatory backend GREEN (22/22). All 19 endpoints validated. Frontend dashboard renders all 8 cards and 3 modals (Budgets, Pricing button visible, Email/SMTP). Two follow-ups: (1) add 5 missing testids on stat cards, (2) confirm alert engine fires on mock data within 24h window or update seed/window. Test suite at /app/backend/tests/test_cost_observatory.py reusable for regression.
